basics.name: Debajyoti Sengupta
basics.label: Machine Learning Research Engineer
basics.summary: Machine Learning Research Engineer building efficient generative systems across physics and neuroscience. Designed and deployed 2B–10B parameter models for multimodal settings, achieving 100× faster simulation than Monte Carlo, real-time closed-loop neural control, and 60% memory reduction with <1% performance loss via QLoRA. Bridges research and production with peer-reviewed work (SciPost, PRD, JHEP) and systems developed in collaboration with industry.

work.name: EPFL & Dandelion Science
work.summary: Building generative control systems for real-time neural optimisation across EEG, MEG, fMRI, and vision modalities.
highlights: Developed and deployed a generative control system mapping neural state deltas to stimulus generation across 5 modalities (EEG, MEG, fMRI, video, image), enabling real-time closed-loop optimisation of neural responses
Scaled multimodal models to ~2B parameters under strict latency constraints, supporting online inference with dual-modality conditioning in control loops
Reduced training memory footprint by ~60% vs full finetuning using QLoRA and quantisation-aware training, with ≤1% degradation in neural prediction accuracy
Learned shared latent representations aligning pretrained vision and neural foundation models, enabling data-efficient cross-modal conditioning under limited neural data regimes
Built production-grade multimodal pipelines for training, alignment, and optimisation, used in collaboration with a Swiss–US NeuroAI company for therapeutic development
Filed patent on generative neural control framework for therapy design

work.name: CERN / University of Geneva
work.summary: PhD research at the intersection of particle physics and generative modelling at CERN.
highlights: Developed CURTAINs, a conditional generative anomaly detection method achieving signal efficiency ~16 with consistent gains over CWoLa/CATHODE, while reducing training time from ~10h to 3h
Designed RAD-OT, an optimal transport reformulation, reducing training time to ≤1h while maintaining detection performance
Designed diffusion-based particle generators (PC-JeDi, PC-Droid) enabling ~100× faster particle simulation than Monte Carlo with ≤1% Wasserstein deviation
Scaled training to TB-scale datasets using distributed multi-node systems (SLURM, 2 nodes, 16×H200 GPUs), sustaining high-throughput training workloads
Increased GPU utilisation from ~16% to 100% by removing data pipeline bottlenecks and restructuring distributed training
Co-recipient of Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics (ATLAS Collaboration)

education.summary: Intersection of particle physics and generative modelling at CERN.

awards.title: Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics
awards.summary: Co-recipient as part of the ATLAS Collaboration.

publications.name: CURTAINs Flows For Flows: Constructing Unobserved Regions with Maximum Likelihood Estimation
publications.summary: Conditional generative anomaly detection for resonance searches. Signal efficiency ~16, consistent gains over CWoLa/CATHODE, training time reduced from 10h to 3h.

publications.name: RAD-OT: Resonance Anomaly Detection with Optimal Transport
publications.summary: Optimal transport reformulation of resonance anomaly detection. Training time ≤1h.

publications.name: PC-Droid: Faster Diffusion and Improved Quality for Particle Cloud Generation
publications.summary: Diffusion-based particle cloud generators, ~100× faster than Monte Carlo with ≤1% Wasserstein deviation.
